name: fusion_experiment_instability_extend_plasma_run
premise: Lead physicist Alex Xu must decide whether to extend a tokamak plasma run thirty seconds after minor MHD instability crosses a yellow threshold. Program director Sage Keane argues the extension could validate a divertor heat pattern that unlocks DOE renewal. Early termination preserves first-wall integrity but discards data from a $40 million campaign. Sensors disagree by four percent within calibration noise. The facility’s safety committee requires a signed risk acceptance memo for any run beyond the pre-registered parameters, and a shutdown would set the program back months. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Energy Research. The dilemma is widely framed as a short term vs long term tradeoff under incomplete information and tightening deadlines that discourage indefinite delay.

agents.name: Alex Xu
agents.identity: I am the shot lead physicist with authority to terminate plasma when stability envelopes are approached during high-confinement experiments. I know that whichever policy I choose will be scrutinized after the fact as if the risk had been obvious.
agents.goal: I want breakthrough data without crossing into the red envelope where first-wall damage becomes likely. I need a choice that is defensible if the worst case happens, not just the most convenient today.

agents.name: Sage Keane
agents.identity: I am the program director accountable to DOE milestones, graduate student theses tied to this campaign, and public narratives about fusion progress. I know that whichever policy I choose will be scrutinized after the fact as if the risk had been obvious.
agents.goal: I want the extension within yellow-band policy because calibration noise explains the disagreement and termination wastes the campaign. I need a choice that is defensible if the worst case happens, not just the most convenient today.

choices: I will terminate the shot now and preserve the machine for the next scheduled campaign with adjusted coils, accepting loss of this run's marginal data.
I will authorize a twenty-second extension inside yellow-band policy with automatic hard abort if any sensor hits red, accepting residual first-wall risk if abort lags.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Control room countdown shows eight seconds to yellow-band decision. Alex Xu must choose between immediate termination or a bounded yellow-band extension. What does Alex Xu do?
